subject: Re: FW: Hello!
body:
call me at hom,e   the phone keeeps ringing when i
call office

--- Lesley Groff <lesley@nysgllc.com> wrote:

>  
> 
> -----Original Message-----
> From: Danielle Prussin
> [mailto:drprussin2000@yahoo.com] 
> Sent: Monday, February 06, 2006 5:06 PM
> To: lesley@nysgllc.com
> Subject: Hello!
> 
> Hi Lesely - 
> 
> Happy Monday!  I hope 2006 has been great for you so
> far...  I have heard
> through Taly that she is waiting on next steps but I
> wanted to reiterate to
> you how excited I still am about the traveling
> assistant position.  I hope
> to be able to work with you!
> 
> I have another note for Jeffrey explaining this --
> would you mind passing it
> along to him?
> 
> Thanks!  Fingers are still crossed!  
> 
> All the best,
> Danielle
> ==================================
> 
> Hi Jeffrey -
> 
> I hope this message finds you well!  I wanted to
> reach out and let you know
> that I continue to be very excited at the prospect
> of working for you.  Your
> traveling assistant position is the job that I want
> and meeting Ghislaine
> and Sara further confirmed that for me.  It offers
> me all of the dimensions
> that I have been seeking, namely environment, team,
> travel, and work
> diversity.
> 
> Over the past two months, I have interviewed for
> other assistant jobs but
> have not found any to be the right fit, primarily
> because they were 'desk
> jobs' or working for/with people I didn't feel a
> connection with.
> I recently stopped interviewing for an Executive
> Assistant position with a
> very successful, high-profile businessman because I
> knew I would not be
> happy working there.  
> 
> Bottom line is that I found my fit with your
> position and am truly hoping
> that you allow me the opportunity to come work for
> you.  I know I will be a
> positive, successful addition to your team!
> 
> Best regards,
> Danielle
> 
> __________________________________________________
> Do You Yahoo!?
> Tired of spam?  Yahoo! Mail has the best spam
> protection around
> http://mail.yahoo.com 
>

body_html:
call me at hom,e the phone keeeps ringing when i
call office

--- Lesley Groff lesley@nysgllc.com wrote:

-----Original Message-----
From: Danielle Prussin
[mailto:drprussin2000@yahoo.com]
Sent: Monday, February 06, 2006 5:06 PM
To: lesley@nysgllc.com
Subject: Hello!

Hi Lesely -

Happy Monday! I hope 2006 has been great for you so
far... I have heard
through Taly that she is waiting on next steps but I
wanted to reiterate to
you how excited I still am about the traveling
assistant position. I hope
to be able to work with you!

I have another note for Jeffrey explaining this --
would you mind passing it
along to him?

Thanks! Fingers are still crossed!

All the best,
Danielle

Hi Jeffrey -

I hope this message finds you well! I wanted to
reach out and let you know
that I continue to be very excited at the prospect
of working for you. Your
traveling assistant position is the job that I want
and meeting Ghislaine
and Sara further confirmed that for me. It offers
me all of the dimensions
that I have been seeking, namely environment, team,
travel, and work
diversity.

Over the past two months, I have interviewed for
other assistant jobs but
have not found any to be the right fit, primarily
because they were 'desk
jobs' or working for/with people I didn't feel a
connection with.
I recently stopped interviewing for an Executive
Assistant position with a
very successful, high-profile businessman because I
knew I would not be
happy working there.

Bottom line is that I found my fit with your
position and am truly hoping
that you allow me the opportunity to come work for
you. I know I will be a
positive, successful addition to your team!

Best regards,
Danielle
